pick_sprint_items-stdout-0002.name: pick_sprint_items.stdout
pick_sprint_items-stdout-0002.data:
Sprint selection written to `docs/sprints/drafts/SPRINT-SELECTION.md`.

**Selected: Items 24 (Humor & Personality Pass) + 25 (Animation Polish)**

The core game loop is complete after Sprint 002. These two items are the natural next step because they both polish the same UI surfaces (animal cards, phase transitions, scoring, shop) and reinforce the same goal — giving the game its "goofy, warm, scrappy" personality through both written voice and visual motion. Doing them together avoids redundant passes through the same code and lets animation and humor decisions inform each other.

Audio (15–23) was excluded because it requires sourcing/generating actual audio files — different work entirely. CI/CD and final QA are end-stage tasks that should come after all content is in place.

merge_sprint-stdout-0007.name: merge_sprint.stdout
merge_sprint-stdout-0007.data:
Written to `docs/sprints/SPRINT-003.md`. Here's what the merged sprint synthesizes:

**Base structure**: Codex draft — strongest architecture (copy separation from catalog, structured `ResolutionEvent[]`, engine purity, exhaustiveness tests, skippable animations).

**CSS specificity**: Claude draft — concrete keyframe names, `--slot-index` staggering, `steps()` retro easing, `data-transition` attributes, micro-interaction details (idle bob, bust shake, purchase wiggle).

**Key decisions from critiques**:
- Transition state stays in UI-local state machine, **not** `GameState` (Gemini critique)
- `animationend` events + timeout fallback instead of pure `setTimeout` (Claude critique)
- All long animations skippable by click/tap/Enter (all critiques)
- Single `copy.ts` organized by domain — compromise between Claude's single file and Codex's three-file split (Gemini critique)
- Win confetti downgraded to stretch goal (Claude critique)
- Character caps on descriptions to prevent overflow (Claude critique)
- `prefe

implement-stdout-0009.name: implement.stdout
implement-stdout-0009.data:
Implemented Sprint 003 end-to-end, including the validation feedback items.

Key changes:
- Centralized all authored copy and removed prose from catalog mechanics: [copy.ts](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/content/copy.ts), [catalog.ts](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/game/catalog.ts)
- Added copy exhaustiveness + length guard tests: [copy.test.ts](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/content/__tests__/copy.test.ts)
- Refactored night summaries to structured events (`ResolutionEvent[]`) with before/after totals: [types.ts](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/game/types.ts), [engine.ts](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/game/engine.ts)
- Added animated summary tally with skip (click/tap/Enter) and final-value `aria-live`: [NightSummaryModal.tsx](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/ui/NightSummaryModal.tsx), [useAnimatedCounter.ts](/Users/caleb.mchenry/code/me/hoot-n-nanny/src/ui/useAnimatedCounter.ts)
- Added local, skippable scene transition
